name: Credential Manager credentials were backed up
definition: Windows Security Event ID 5376 records successful backup of the user Credential Manager database.
trigger_scenarios: This event is generated when the Subject successfully backs up the Credential Manager database, typically through the Credential Manager control-panel workflow.

key_fields.explanation: Identifies the account that performed the backup operation. Microsoft documents that this is the Subject account.
Hexadecimal logon ID that correlates the backup to the user logon session and nearby process creation.
Identifies whether the backup occurred on a domain controller, member server, or workstation; Microsoft documents that the event can occur on all three.

false_positives: Legitimate user-initiated credential backup through Credential Manager is possible but rare.
Help-desk migration workflows can intentionally back up user credentials during device replacement.
detection_notes: Use threshold=1 occurrence because Microsoft states every 5376 should be recorded and that Credential Manager backup is very rarely used and can indicate malicious activity. MITRE T1555.004 explicitly notes credential backups via rundll32.exe keymgr.dll KRShowKeyMgr and Windows Credential Manager vault material under %Systemdrive%\Users\[Username]\AppData\Local\Microsoft\[Vault/Credentials]\. Correlate SubjectLogonId to 4688 for rundll32.exe, vaultcmd.exe, or unusual interactive activity before closing.
kql_snippet:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 5376
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account
sample_log:
EventID: 5376
SubjectUserName: j.smith
SubjectDomainName: CORP
SubjectLogonId: 0x30d7c
Computer: WS-042

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Security Event ID 5376
queries.query:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 5376
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, EventID, RenderedDescription
| order by TimeGenerated desc
assumptions: The Windows Security Events connector populates SecurityEvent.
Map raw XML fields to the event-specific fields described on this page.

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Security Event ID 5376
queries.query:
index=wineventlog source="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=5376
| table _time, host, EventCode, Message
assumptions: Index, source, and parsed field names vary by deployment.
Apply the event-specific field conditions after validating local field extraction.

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Security Event ID 5376
assumptions: The Windows Security or applicable Windows event channel is ingested into Cortex XDR.
Inspect raw provider fields because parsed names vary by collection path.

queries.title: Windows Security Event ID 5376
queries.query:
title: Windows Security Event ID 5376
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: security
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 5376
    condition: selection
falsepositives:

Add the documented field conditions and correlations in the destination SIEM.

value_references.meaning: Credential Manager credentials were backed up successfully.
value_references.security_relevance: Use EventID together with the other documented event fields and correlated activity; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

value_references.meaning: The documented event XML marks this as a successful Security audit event.
value_references.security_relevance: Use Keywords together with the other documented event fields and correlated activity; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

faqs.question: What does Windows Event ID 5376 mean?
faqs.answer: A user successfully backed up the Credential Manager database.

faqs.question: Does Event ID 5376 always indicate malicious activity?
faqs.answer: It is legitimate but rare; Microsoft recommends recording every occurrence because malicious software may invoke the function.

faqs.question: How should Event ID 5376 be investigated?
faqs.answer: Resolve SubjectLogonId to its logon and process activity, then validate the user migration or backup change record.

sources.title: 5376: Credential Manager credentials were backed up
